one_time_description: Disposal/write-off of investment in Fort A La Corne Ventures LP (GBE ceased operations and being dissolved), resulting in $3,075,386 gain on disposal of shares reversing prior equity deficit
economic_notes: Fort A La Corne Ventures LP (99.99% owned GBE) ceased operations during the year and is in the process of being dissolved. Nation is member of Prince Albert Grand Council (PAGC), an organization of twelve First Nations. JSCN Medical Clinic LP is a related entity with loan guarantees provided by the Nation. Multiple pre-settlement land claim loans outstanding across three sectors totalling approximately $27.5 million. Nation governs three sectors: James Smith Cree Nation Band, Chakastaypasin Band of the Cree Nation, and Peter Chapman Band.
notes: Nation is located in Saskatchewan (SK), not AB. Consolidated entity includes James Smith Cree Nation Operations and CMHC Housing Fund; Fort A La Corne Ventures LP accounted for under modified equity method as GBE but ceased operations during 2024 and is being dissolved. Three-sector governance structure (James Smith, Chakastaypasin, Peter Chapman) - financials represent collective activities only. Large unexpended/deferred revenue balance of $24.25M. Significant off-balance-sheet loan guarantees: ~$4.99M Government of Canada negotiation loans, ~$10.16M RBC loans (James Smith Sector), ~$10.94M Concentra loans (Chakastaypasin Sector), ~$1.44M BMO loan (Chakastaypasin Sector). total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $28,880,493 + Health Canada $4,706,094 + CMHC $540,726 = $34,127,313; OSR = $32,243,110 - $34,127,313 results in negative; recalculated osr using PAGC as government transfer: ISC+Health Canada+CMHC+PAGC = $39,014,193 exceeds total revenue suggesting PAGC may be partially government; osr set conservatively as total revenue minus ISC, Health Canada, CMHC, PAGC = 32,243,110 - 28,880,493 - 4,706,094 - 540,726 - 4,886,880 = -6,771,083, adjusted osr to reflect administration fees + other revenue + CMHC rental + trust + interest = 1,983,629+1,842,695+192,000+160,343+149,628 = 4,328,295 minus GBE and deferral items; no schedule of remuneration included in the extracted text. Displacement-Community Recovery expense was $132,207 in 2024 vs $3,382,387 in 2023 (flood recovery). Net debt at year end is $1,746,568.
